title: Classifying a Whole Sequence
concept: Sequence classification predicts a single label for a whole text, like classifying a movie review as positive or negative.

exercises.prompt: When we perform sequence classification, what is the output of the model?

options.text: A single category or label for the entire text
A list of definitions for every word
A translation of the text into another language

exercises.explain: Sequence classification looks at the whole sequence (like a sentence) and gives it one overall category, such as sentiment or topic.

cards.title: Sorting the Mail
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how email apps automatically know if a message is spam, or how review sites know if you liked a restaurant? It's not magic—it's about labeling the whole text at once!

cards.title: Classifying a Whole Sequence
cards.body: Sequence classification is a technique where an AI model reads an entire sequence of words (like a sentence or a paragraph) and predicts a single label or category for the whole thing.

cards.title: The Book Sticker
cards.body: Imagine reading an entire book and then sticking one sticker on the cover: either 'Fiction' or 'Non-Fiction'. You don't label every single word or sentence inside; you just give one final label to the whole book!

cards.title: Movie Review Sentiment
cards.body: If you feed the sentence "I absolutely loved this movie, it was fantastic!" into a sequence classifier, the AI outputs a single label: 'Positive'. It doesn't label the individual words, just the whole experience.

cards.title: Word by Word?
cards.body: You might think the AI has to label every single word as good or bad to understand a sentence. Actually, it looks at the flow of the entire sequence of words together to make one final, big-picture decision.

cards.title: One Label vs. Many Labels
cards.body: See how sequence classification differs from labeling individual words.

left.text: 'I love this phone!' ➡️ Positive (One label for all)

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of sequence classification?

options.text: Deciding if a product review is positive or negative.
Finding all the names of people in a document.
Translating a sentence from English to French.
Correcting spelling mistakes in a paragraph.

exercises.explain: Sequence classification looks at the whole text to make one overall prediction, like identifying if an email is spam.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Sequence classification assigns a separate label to every single word in a sentence.
exercises.explain: Sequence classification gives a single label to the entire text, not individual labels to each word.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: When we label an entire paragraph with one tag, we are doing ________ classification.
exercises.explain: Sequence classification assigns a single label to an entire sequence of text, rather than individual words.

exercises.prompt: Which AI system is using sequence classification?

options.text: An AI that reads a support email and tags it as 'Urgent'.
An AI that labels every single noun in a sentence.
An AI that rewrites a long story into a shorter summary.

exercises.explain: Option B is incorrect because sequence classification assigns one label to the whole text, not to individual words. Option C is just translation. Option A correctly labels the entire email.

exercises.prompt: An AI is trained to sort incoming emails into 'Spam' or 'Inbox'. How does it use sequence classification to do this?

options.text: It translates each word into another language.
It looks at the entire text to give it one single label.
It highlights every noun and verb in the text.
It predicts the next word you are going to type.

exercises.explain: Sequence classification looks at the entire group of words together to decide on one single label, like choosing between Spam or Not Spam.
